questions.q: What is meant by reconstitution of a partnership?
questions.answer: Reconstitution of a partnership refers to the changes made in the existing partnership firm due to retirement or death of a partner.

questions.q: State the reasons for reconstitution of a partnership firm.
questions.answer: Reasons for reconstitution of a partnership firm are retirement of a partner, death of a partner, change in profit sharing ratio, admission of new partner, etc.

questions.q: What is the effect of retirement of a partner on the capital of the firm?
questions.answer: Retirement of a partner reduces the capital of the firm as the retiring partner's share of capital is withdrawn from the firm.

questions.q: What is the method of valuation of goodwill at the time of retirement of a partner?
questions.answer: The method of valuation of goodwill at the time of retirement of a partner is average profit method or super profit method.

questions.q: What is the effect of death of a partner on the capital of the firm?
questions.answer: Death of a partner increases the capital of the firm as the amount received by the executor of the deceased partner is invested in the firm.

questions.q: State the method of valuation of goodwill at the time of death of a partner.
questions.answer: The method of valuation of goodwill at the time of death of a partner is average profit method or super profit method.

questions.q: X and Y are partners sharing profits and losses in the ratio 3:1. X retires and his share is taken over by Z. What will be the new profit sharing ratio?
questions.answer: The new profit sharing ratio will be 2:1 (1 part of X's share is taken over by Z, so the remaining 2 parts will be retained by Y).

questions.q: A and B are partners sharing profits and losses in the ratio 5:3. A dies and his share is taken over by C. What will be the new profit sharing ratio?
questions.answer: The new profit sharing ratio will be 5:4 (3 parts of A's share are taken over by C, so the remaining 5 parts will be retained by B).

questions.q: A and B are partners sharing profits and losses in the ratio 7:5. A retires and his share is taken over by C. Calculate the amount of goodwill to be transferred to the new partner C.
questions.answer: The amount of goodwill to be transferred to the new partner C can be calculated using the average profit method or super profit method.

questions.q: A and B are partners sharing profits and losses in the ratio 9:6. A dies and his share is taken over by C. Calculate the amount of goodwill to be transferred to the new partner C.
questions.answer: The amount of goodwill to be transferred to the new partner C can be calculated using the average profit method or super profit method.

questions.q: X and Y are partners sharing profits and losses in the ratio 4:3. X retires and his share is taken over by Z. Calculate the amount of goodwill to be transferred to the new partner Z.
questions.answer: The amount of goodwill to be transferred to the new partner Z can be calculated using the average profit method or super profit method.

questions.q: A and B are partners sharing profits and losses in the ratio 6:8. A dies and his share is taken over by C. Calculate the amount of goodwill to be transferred to the new partner C.
questions.answer: The amount of goodwill to be transferred to the new partner C can be calculated using the average profit method or super profit method.

questions.q: What is the effect of reconstitution of a partnership on the capital of the firm?
questions.answer: Reconstitution of a partnership affects the capital of the firm by changing the proportion of the partners' capitals.

questions.q: What are the methods of valuation of goodwill at the time of reconstitution of a partnership?
questions.answer: The methods of valuation of goodwill at the time of reconstitution of a partnership are average profit method and super profit method.

questions.q: What is the effect of reconstitution of a partnership on the profits of the firm?
questions.answer: Reconstitution of a partnership affects the profits of the firm by changing the proportion of the partners' shares in the profits.

questions.q: What are the steps involved in reconstituting a partnership firm?
questions.answer: The steps involved in reconstituting a partnership firm are: settlement of the retiring or deceased partner's share, valuation of goodwill, and adjustment of capital and profits.

questions.q: What is the difference between the method of reconstitution of a partnership firm due to retirement of a partner and death of a partner?
questions.answer: The method of reconstitution of a partnership firm due to retirement of a partner involves the transfer of the retiring partner's share to the remaining partners, whereas the method of reconstitution of a partnership firm due to death of a partner involves the transfer of the deceased partner's share to the executor or the remaining partners.
